candidates.evidence_quote: Comparable sales for the second quarter increased 1.5% from the prior year and 14.1% versus 2019.
North Italia's second quarter comparable sales increased a solid 8% from the prior year and 30% versus 2019
the pricing was about 10.5% roughly for the quarter. The mix was a negative 5.4%, and then the traffic was a negative 3.7%.
cost of sales decreased 130 basis points, driven by higher menu pricing than commodity inflation
The mix was a negative 5.4%
we expect effective commodity inflation of low single digits for Q3. as our broad market basket continues to stabilize
despite lapping favorable dairy contracts in the second quarter of 2022
We are modeling net total labor inflation of about mid single digits when factoring in the latest trends in wage rates
we're seeing also wage inflation rates that are below pre-pandemic levels
including labor productivity and wage management, driving solid flow through for improved profitability
take advantage of the better staffing and execution that David Gordon talked about
another quarter of strong staff retention, with restaurant management and hourly staff attrition rates effectively returning back to our industry-leading pre-pandemic levels
second quarter guest satisfaction scores at the Cheesecake Factory improving both sequentially and year over year
on-premise incident rates remained above 2019 levels, with no material change to day part mix
Cheesecake Factory off-premise sales for the second quarter totaled 22% of sales, just below first quarter levels
Delivery still makes up about 9% to 10% of that mix
a very strong commission rate that means that all of our off-premise sales, including the DoorDash sales, are not in any way margin dilutive
the program is off to a promising start with member enrollment surpassing our expectations
partially offset by marketing costs related to launching the rewards program
our marketing strategy to tie our creative marketing campaigns to on-brand events to generate publicity and increase consumer engagement
Our focus on menu innovation remains a key point of differentiation contributing to our broad consumer appeal and high degree of relevancy
the Cheesecake Factory restaurants continue to generate industry-leading annualized unit volumes averaging 12.4 million for the quarter
external bakery sales were $15.4 million during the second quarter of fiscal 2023
CapEx totaled approximately $25 million during the second quarter for new unit development and maintenance
we continue to experience some delays in opening dates due to various challenges primarily beyond our control, particularly construction and permit approval delays
Pre-opening costs were $6 million in the quarter compared to $2.9 million in the prior year period

We anticipate net income margin of approximately 2.75% for Q3 at the midpoint of the revenue range
we anticipate total revenues to be between $835 and $855 million
Total revenues of $866.2 million were just under the low end of the range

we completed approximately $9.3 million in share repurchases
returned just over $13.8 million to shareholders via our dividend
we now anticipate approximately $160 million to $170 million in CapEx to support this year's and some of next year's unit development
we recorded a net expense of $0.6 million related to impairment of assets and lease termination income and FRC acquisition related expenses
really May was a little bit softer, maybe a couple of percent in terms of the timing and trying to figure out that seasonality of the new normal

notes: Chunk = 1 event only: Q2 FY2023 earnings-call transcript part 1 of 2 (prepared remarks + early Q&A); part 2 handled by another bot.
fiscal_kpis was null for this chunk, so all candidates are transcript-grounded.

Skipped (8): consumer sentiment/behavior shifts (bare-category/vague); summer heat hurting patio usage (speaker speculative: 'I don't know if it's possible'); Fox Restaurant Concepts (FRC) average weekly sales (subsidiary legal-name risk); 'revenge buying' post-COVID surge (one-off prior-year anomaly, metaphor wording); Fortune best-workplaces recognition (provider label, vague causal link); liquidity/debt balances (static balance-sheet facts, no causal claim); applicant flow per hiring need (folded into restaurant_staffing); 6.3-6.5%/7% unit growth targets ('growth' banned; cause covered by new_unit_development).
All 37 evidence quotes verified verbatim substrings of the event content.
